title: Build script skips TypeScript checking
reasoning: The reviewed feature is defined as package script build running `tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build`, but the actual `build` script only invokes `electron-vite build`. A developer, release job, or package consumer running `pnpm build` can produce release artifacts without the explicit TypeScript no-emit check that the feature promises. `check:ci` runs typecheck separately, but that does not protect the `build` entrypoint itself.
reproduction: Run `pnpm build`; it executes `electron-vite build` directly rather than `tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build`.
recommendation: Change the build script to `tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build`, or make `build` delegate to the existing `typecheck` script before invoking `electron-vite build`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for this package script behavior, and package scripts are not validated by the listed Vitest test suite evidence.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a package-script assertion that `scripts.build` includes the typecheck step before `electron-vite build`, or add a CI smoke test that runs `pnpm build` against a fixture with a TypeScript error and verifies it fails before bundling.
